premortem.draft: Over the next two fiscal years the investment loses money if subscription growth decelerates while AI and platform investment pressure margins, causing the market to reject the SaaS base-rate valuation path.
premortem.source_label: C-4 DCF scenarios and C-5 edge cruxes
premortem.excerpt_or_summary: Scenario and edge-crux artifacts identify subscription growth, NOPAT margin, sales-to-capital, and platform attach as the key risks.

bear_case_narrative.draft: A credible short seller's bear case is that Salesforce is a maturing enterprise software platform: because growth normalizes, AI spending raises opex, acquisitions and stock compensation dilute per-share economics, and competition weakens expansion, downside can persist as estimates reset below the SaaS sector base case.
bear_case_narrative.excerpt_or_summary: The filed bear scenario quantifies lower value when SaaS growth, margin, and sales-to-capital assumptions are weaker.

modellable_risks.risk: Subscription revenue growth falls below the filed SaaS base scenario path.
modellable_risks.modeled_effect: Lower revenue growth flows through the DCF revenue driver and reduces per-share value.
modellable_risks.excerpt_or_summary: The scenario artifact binds bear/base/bull values to SaaS revenue-growth assumptions.

modellable_risks.risk: AI, go-to-market, or competitive pressure compresses incremental operating margins.
modellable_risks.modeled_effect: Lower NOPAT margin reduces the DCF value and conviction score.
modellable_risks.source_label: C-2 moat and C-3 capital allocation
modellable_risks.excerpt_or_summary: Moat and capital allocation evidence frame product investment, competition, and margin durability as central SaaS tests.

tail_risks.risk: A major data security, privacy, or AI governance incident damages trust in the platform.
tail_risks.why_not_modelled: The timing, customer churn impact, legal remedy, and cross-product reputational effect are discontinuous rather than responsibly estimable with a simple probability-weighted margin haircut.
tail_risks.monitoring_signal: Material breach disclosure, regulatory order, customer-trust event, or AI governance restriction affecting core clouds.
tail_risks.source_label: C-1 business and C-5 edge cruxes
tail_risks.excerpt_or_summary: Business and edge artifacts frame data trust and platform attach as non-linear thesis risks.

kill_metric.thesis_action: Force Senior re-underwrite of the SaaS base-rate crux and move to pass unless margin or attach evidence offsets broken growth durability.
kill_metric.excerpt_or_summary: The subscription growth crux is the central falsifiable monitor for the variant view.

risk_completeness.draft: The sheet is decision-ready for M3.6 because the main downside path, DCF bear value, modelled risks, and tail bucket are tied to filed route artifacts. Could not verify real-time renewal rates, AI attach, or current-quarter enterprise software budgets beyond authored fixtures. Sustained subscription growth with stable margins would raise confidence; growth below the SaaS bear path or margin compression would lower confidence.
risk_completeness.excerpt_or_summary: Filed artifacts provide enough evidence for an undecided Senior review package while preserving explicit gaps.

source_evidence_summary.business: Business artifact frames CRM as a subscription-heavy enterprise cloud software platform.
source_evidence_summary.moat: Moat artifact supplies switching-cost and platform-breadth context.
source_evidence_summary.capalloc: Capital allocation artifact supplies reinvestment, dilution, and balance-sheet context.
source_evidence_summary.scenarios: Scenario artifact supplies filed SaaS-sector DCF bear/base/bull paths and bear value.
source_evidence_summary.edge_cruxes: Edge & Cruxes artifact supplies falsifiable route-specific thesis monitors.
source_evidence_summary.mechanical: Gate card, method directive, DCF valuation, reverse DCF, and spine provide mechanical downside context.
